channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The Rise of Deepfakes in Politics: Can Chrome Extension Hiya Save the Day?
item.description: Deepfakes have become a significant concern in the 2024 presidential election, but a new Chrome extension, Hiya, claims to be able to detect fake audio clips with high accuracy.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: AI-generated child sex abuse images pose challenges for federal prosecutors
item.description: U.S. federal prosecutors are stepping up their pursuit of suspects who use artificial intelligence tools to manipulate or create child sex abuse images, as law enforcement fears the technology could spur a flood of illicit material. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Alabama man arrested in SEC social media account hack that led the price of bitcoin to spike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An Alabama man was arrested Thursday for his alleged role in the January hack of a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission social media account that led the price of bitcoin to spike, the Justice Department said. Eric Council Jr.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Robinhood launches desktop platform, seeks market share from traditional brokerages
item.description: Robinhood launched its long-awaited desktop platform and added futures and index options trading features to its mobile app on Wednesday, as the fintech firm aims to take market share from traditional brokerages.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Netflix's subscriber growth is slowing, but its profit and stock price are still surging
item.description: Netflix says its subscriber growth slowed dramatically during the summer, a sign that the huge gains from the video-streaming service’s crackdown on freeloading viewers is tapering off.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: HPE, Dell launch another round of AI servers
item.description: The AI spin continues, with Hewlett Packard Enterprise and Dell Technologies both introducing new servers oriented towards training large language models (LLM).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Rep. Tlaib calls out Kroger for facial recognition practices
item.description: Kroger said it uses facial recognition to present targeted ads on its digital price tags that it rolled out five years ago.
item.dc:creator: Kelsey Kernstine, NewsNation

item.title: How Wikipedia is staying relevant in the AI era
item.description: Despite the rise of such AI models as ChatGPT and Google Gemini, “old-school” Wikipedia remains one of the most visited sites around the world—with 15 billion visitors every month.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: FTC announces rule to make it easier to cancel subscriptions
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission is taking steps to make it easier for consumers to cancel digital subscriptions.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Adobe starts roll-out of AI video tools, challenging OpenAI and Meta
item.description: Facing much larger rivals, Adobe has staked its future on building models trained on data that it has rights to use, ensuring the output can be legally used in commercial work.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: NCSC seeks to expand counterintelligence outreach
item.description: A new national strategy warns that counterintelligence pros have to work more closely with other agencies and the private sector to address threats. The post NCSC seeks to expand counterintelligence outreach first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft settles video gamers' lawsuit over Activision takeover
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Microsoft Corp. has settled a lawsuit from a group of gamers who sued to try to stop the company from buying video game publisher Activision Blizzard for $69 billion last year. Terms of the settlement were not disclosed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mexico says foreign firms have pledged $20 billion in investments, but many are old or uncertain
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican officials announced Tuesday what they claimed was $20 billion in new foreign direct investment in Mexico, but much of that was neither new, nor completely certain.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: How to choose a flexible unified SASE solution
item.description: SASE is at the top of all CIOs’ minds, and for good reason. When deployed properly, SASE, which combines SD-WAN with cloud-delivered security via SSE, enables all users and devices, regardless of location, to securely access an organization’s hybrid network.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Tech consultant goes on trial in death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The tech consultant charged in Cash App founder Bob Lee's stabbing death had no motive to kill him and in fact was forced to defend himself against Lee, who had become aggressive while on a multiday drug bender, lawyers for Nima Momeni said in opening statements Monday.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Adobe makes GenStudio app generally available
item.description: Adobe’s GenStudio content supply chain platform is now generally available, with the ability to publish content directly to social media channels such as Instagram, Snap and TikTok coming soon.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Musk's Starlink Internet free for hurricane victims through 2024
item.description: Following criticism, the company issued a new offer that extends the free service through the end of the year for victims of both hurricanes Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: AI is killing cloud sustainability
item.description: Sustainability was once a highly touted advantage in public cloud computing. Enterprises and cloud providers flaunted their green initiatives, promoting data centers powered by renewable energy that would reduce carbon footprints. This topic has quietly slipped off the radar in recent months.
item.dc:creator: Info World
